data.title: Chainlink Dominates DeFi as Qubetics and Hedera Surge in Crypto Market
data.description:
In the rapidly evolving crypto market, three major projects have emerged as leaders in innovation and expansion: Qubetics, Hedera, and Chainlink. Each of these projects is making significant strides in their respective areas, attracting attention from investors and developers alike.

Qubetics, with its Web3 innovation, is simplifying blockchain development through its QubeQode IDE. This platform allows developers to deploy blockchain solutions without extensive coding knowledge, making it easier for businesses and individuals to implement blockchain-based applications. The QubeQode IDE features a drag-and-drop interface, enabling users to create smart contracts, tokenize assets, and set up DeFi solutions with minimal technical expertise. This innovation is particularly beneficial for small businesses and indie developers who lack the resources to hire specialized development teams.

Qubetics' presale is currently in its 24th stage, with the token priced at $0.0976. The presale has raised over $14.7 million, with more than 22,500 token holders and 497 million $TICS tokens sold. The presale operates in 7-day stages, with a 10% price increase every Sunday at midnight. This structure provides early participants with the opportunity to secure tokens before they hit exchanges, potentially leading to significant returns on investment. For instance, a $1,000 investment at the current price could turn into over $100,000 if $TICS reaches $10 after launch. With the mainnet launch set for Q2 2025, Qubetics is positioning itself as a top crypto investment opportunity.

Hedera Hashgraph is also making significant moves with its billion-dollar expansion. The Hedera Council has approved a 4.86 billion HBAR token allocation to support network development and decentralized governance initiatives. This funding will be distributed to key entities, including the HBAR Foundation and the Hashgraph Association, to drive adoption and ecosystem growth. Hedera's governance model is being strengthened with the addition of major players from tech and academia, such as google, University College London, and Wipro. This ensures that the network's development remains transparent, efficient, and innovative. The question now is whether this funding injection will drive new momentum for HBAR's price and adoption.

Chainlink, on the other hand, continues to dominate the DeFi space with its secure, real-world

data.title: Dawgz AI surges 200% in presale as AI meme coin outpaces rivals
data.description:
In the rapidly evolving world of cryptocurrency, identifying the next big project that could dominate the market is a challenge that many investors and analysts are eager to tackle. The current trends in the crypto space are heavily influenced by meme coins and AI-powered tokens, which are gaining traction due to their unique blend of community engagement and real-world utility.

One of the standout projects in this space is Dawgz AI ($DAGZ), a meme coin that leverages AI-driven trading bots to generate passive income for its holders. Unlike many meme coins that rely solely on viral marketing, Dawgz AI offers a more substantial value proposition by integrating AI technology into its trading strategy. This allows holders to earn passive income without the need for manual trading, making it an attractive option for those looking to capitalize on the growing interest in both meme coins and AI-driven projects.

Dawgz AI's AI-powered trading bots analyze market trends and execute trades automatically, providing a competitive edge in the volatile crypto market. The project has already raised over $2.5 million in its presale, indicating strong early adoption and momentum. With a total supply of 8.888 billion tokens, Dawgz AI is designed for long-term scarcity-driven growth, positioning it as a strong contender for the next big crypto in 2025.

In addition to Dawgz AI, other projects are also making waves in the crypto space. Floki Inu ($FLOKI), named after Elon Musk’s dog, has built a strong community and is expanding into NFTs and metaverse gaming. This project goes beyond the typical meme coin by offering actual ecosystem growth, making it more than just a viral token. Similarly, Pepe 2.0 ($PEPE2) is a revival of the PEPE coin craze with updated tokenomics and stronger community engagement, while Kitty AI ($KITTYAI) aims to change the meme space with AI-powered machine learning and community rewards. Bonk ($BONK) is the first Solana-based meme coin to gain massive traction, thanks to airdrops and strong community incentives.

When comparing Dawgz AI to other top meme coins, it stands out due to its AI-powered trading capabilities, staking rewards, and strong presale momentum. While Dogecoin (DOGE) and Shiba Inu (SHIB) have already established themselves as large-cap coins

data.title: Solaxy Crypto Presale Surges 30% as Investors Flock to Layer 2 Blockchain
data.description:
Crypto presales have historically offered some of the most promising investment opportunities, allowing investors to start accumulating tokens before they officially list on exchanges at a lower price. However, finding presales that emphasize investor security and can generate the momentum to propel them to explode on launch isn’t easy.

Solaxy (SOLX) is one of the hottest crypto presales currently on the market. With 30% of the total SOLX supply dedicated to developing its ecosystem, Solaxy seeks to provide investors with faster transactions and lower gas fees via its Layer 2 blockchain. The Solaxy Layer 2 will use a system called rollups that will bundle and process transactions before finalizing them on Solana’s mainnet, reducing the burden on Solana’s Layer 1 while retaining Solana’s security and transparency. Launched in December 2024, the project entered March with $24 million raised and over 75,000 followers across X (Twitter) and Telegram, making it one of the most popular presales today.

Impressed by Solaxy’s focus on community growth, crypto expert Danjo Capital Master pointed out that the project’s developers are constantly providing its supporters with updates on the development of their Layer 2 solution. The latest dev update also revealed the team behind Solaxy intends to include a token launchpad in Solaxy’s ecosystem, which is reminiscent of Pepe Unchained’s (PEPU) project that saw its price soar by over 700% just 48 hours into its launch. Danjo Capital Master encourages his audience to enter the presale during its early stages, as the per-token price of SOLX will only rise until its first DEX listing. Additionally, the expert pointed out that the project offers a high staking APY, allowing investors to passively earn more Solaxy.

Meme Index (MEMEX) seeks to reinvent meme coin investment with its upcoming decentralized investment platform, allowing its users to make pooled investments via four different meme coin indexes. The value of the investment will change based on the performance of all the tokens in the “basket,” and investors will have a chance to decide between low-to-high volatility tokens. The platform will launch with the Titan (leading meme coins), Moonshot (top contenders), Midcap (slightly volatile tokens), and Frenzy (highly volatile coins) indexes.

data.title: Macro headwinds on Wall Street, plus jobs report, crypto summit
NFP Preview: A Major Test for U.S. Markets Amid Volatility
data.description:
On Friday night, the U.S. February Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP) and unemployment rate report will be released. The strength or weakness of this report will directly impact the Federal Reserve's March dot plot and future rate expectations.

Currently, U.S. stocks are experiencing a period of turbulence. The once high-flying Magnificent Seven and the Nasdaq Composite have underperformed global markets in 2025. In such uncertain times, investors are relying on economic data for guidance.

Recent employment indicators suggest that the U.S. labor market is cooling significantly:

The adp Employment Report, often referred to as a mini NFP, showed that February's private payrolls increased by only 77,000, far below the expected 140,000.

Additionally, February job cuts surged to 172,000, a sharp rise from 50,000 in January.

How Will NFP Impact the Markets?

The market's reaction to the NFP report will depend on whether the data exceeds, meets, or falls short of expectations:

If NFP exceeds expectations, unemployment falls below 4%, recession fears ease, stocks and the U.S. dollar strengthen, Fed rate cut expectations decline, and gold and silver face downward pressure.

If NFP and unemployment meet expectations, limited market impact.

If NFP disappoints, and unemployment rises above expectations, recession fears intensify, stocks and the U.S. dollar decline sharply, gold and silver will surge.

Wall Street's NFP Forecasts

Goldman Sachs: NFP Estimate: +170,000, Unemployment Rate: 4.0%, Average Hourly Earnings (MoM): +0.3%

Key Points: Solid job growth supported by pent-up hiring demand and immigration. However, federal job cuts (layoffs, hiring freezes, delayed resignations) could reduce payrolls by 10,000 jobs, though the impact is manageable. Strikes could have a net drag of 5,000 jobs.

Weather Impact: Colder-than-usual temperatures in February, but reduced snowfall (seasonally adjusted) means a neutral impact on employment.

Wage Growth Outlook:+0.3% MoM, reflecting weakening wage pressures but with some calendar-based support.

Bank of America (BofA): NFP Estimate: +185,000, Unemployment Rate: 4.0%,  Average Hourly Earnings (MoM): +0.3%

Key Points: Federal hiring freeze could slightly reduce government employment, which typically averages 25,000 monthly job additions. DOGE-driven job cuts are unlikely to have a significant impact on February's report.

Weather Impact: February's temperatures were below average, meaning cold weather could pose some downside risks.

Work Hours Impact: Average weekly hours are expected to rise to 34.2, mechanically lowering hourly earnings to 0.3% MoM.

Inflation vs. Wage Growth: Wage growth remains above inflation, meaning consumers' purchasing power is increasing, supporting economic expansion despite slower job growth.

Barclays: NFP Estimate: +150,000,  Unemployment Rate: 4.0%,  Average Hourly Earnings (MoM): +0.3%

Key Points: Federal layoffs are expected to have a moderate impact, while private-sector job gains are estimated at 130,000, slightly above January's 113,000. 

Labor market risks remain mixed: Some models suggest upside potential, while policy uncertainty poses downside hiring risks.

Unemployment Rate: DOGE-related layoffs occurred too late to affect February's jobless rate. While government layoffs could introduce some volatility, the unemployment trend remains downward. Slower immigration growth in 2025 could gradually pressure labor supply expansion.

Wage Growth Outlook: +0.3% MoM and 4.0% YoY, reflecting steady wage gains. Average weekly hours are expected to rise slightly to 34.2.

data.title: Ainvest Calendar | Check Out These Top Events For Next Week
Yen surges as safe haven amid US-Japan tensions and global trust shift
data.description:
In recent geopolitical developments, Japan's yen has emerged as a new safe haven currency, reflecting a shift in global trust away from U.S. treaties. This change is driven by growing criticism and mistrust towards the United States, particularly from countries that have traditionally relied on U.S. treaties for security and economic stability. Japan's ambassador to Tehran has voiced strong criticism against the secondary sanctions imposed by the United States on countries engaging in business with Iran, highlighting the increasing tension between the U.S. and other nations.

The concept of "exorbitant privilege" refers to the advantages the United States enjoys due to the U.S. dollar being the international reserve currency. This privilege has allowed the U.S. to maintain a dominant position in global finance. However, recent events have raised questions about the sustainability of this status. The criticism from Japan's ambassador and the shift towards the yen as a safe haven currency suggest that other nations are seeking alternatives to the U.S. dollar, potentially eroding the U.S.'s financial dominance.

U.S. President Donald Trump has also expressed dissatisfaction with the Japan-U.S. security treaty, stating that while the U.S. is committed to protecting Japan, the same level of commitment is not reciprocated. This sentiment further underscores the strained relationship between the two countries and the potential for a shift in global alliances. The yen's rise as a safe haven currency is a direct response to these geopolitical tensions, as investors seek stability in a volatile global landscape.

data.title: XRP Consolidates Above 196 Amid Market Fear and Uncertainty
data.description:
CryptoBull, a well-known crypto analyst, recently highlighted XRP’s prolonged consolidation phase, noting that the cryptocurrency has been trading within a specific range for 95 days while holding above $1.96. The analyst suggested that the current market sentiment is marked by fear, pessimism, and uncertainty, but that XRP could continue its upward trajectory once broader market enthusiasm increases.

The attached chart illustrates XRP trading within a well-defined range, roughly between $1.96 and $3.00, for over three months. The price action suggests a phase of accumulation or indecision, where buyers and sellers remain relatively balanced. While XRP has maintained support above $1.96, it has yet to break out decisively above the upper boundary.

CryptoBull’s assertion about market uncertainty and future price appreciation was met with skepticism by some users. One respondent dismissed the analysis, stating that the obvious should not be hyped up without immediate results. Another user offered a more critical perspective, alleging market manipulation and suggesting that technical indicators, such as bearish divergences, indicate potential downside risks.

CryptoBull’s comment that XRP is in a phase of fear, pessimism, and uncertainty aligns with broader market conditions. Despite XRP’s price stability, overall sentiment in the crypto space remains fragile, influenced by factors such as regulatory developments, macroeconomic trends, and Bitcoin’s price movements. Historically, extended consolidation periods often precede significant price movements, but whether this results in an upward breakout or a breakdown remains uncertain.

Jack Goff’s mention of bearish divergences suggests that technical indicators may not fully support an immediate bullish move. If momentum indicators such as the RSI or MACD show declining strength while the price remains stable or rises, it could indicate weakening buying pressure. In such cases, a correction may follow before any sustained uptrend materializes.

Crypto market analyst Egrag Crypto has set ambitious price targets for XRP, predicting a potential 718% rally that could see the asset reach $27. This optimistic outlook is based on the assumption that XRP could repeat its past performance, which saw significant gains in a relatively short period. The analyst's projection suggests that if XRP follows a similar trajectory, it could experience a substantial surge in value.

data.title: "Tammy Nam Takes the Helm at Creatopy: A New Era for AI-Powered Ads"
data.description:
In the fast-paced world of AI-driven marketing, Creatopy has made a bold move by appointing Tammy H. nam as its new CEO. Nam, a seasoned executive with a proven track record in scaling startups and a deep understanding of marketing technology, brings a wealth of experience to the table. Her previous roles as COO and CMO at PicsArt and CEO of Viki have equipped her with the skills necessary to navigate the complexities of the tech industry and drive Creatopy towards unprecedented growth.

Nam's appointment comes at a pivotal moment for Creatopy, which has seen a 400% increase in mid-market and enterprise revenue between February 2024 and February 2025. This remarkable growth is a testament to the company's innovative approach to AI-powered ad creation, which has attracted major enterprise customers such as astrazeneca, NASCAR, and The Economist. Nam's strategic vision and leadership will be crucial in sustaining this momentum and ensuring that Creatopy continues to thrive in the competitive landscape of AI-powered marketing solutions.

One of the key factors driving Creatopy's success is its focus on brand safety and high-touch customer service. Nam has emphasized the importance of brand safety, stating that "brand safety is a top priority" for the company. This commitment is evident in the way Creatopy ensures that every AI-generated ad adheres to the brand guidelines uploaded by marketing managers during account setup. This approach not only enhances brand safety but also builds customer trust and loyalty, which are essential for long-term success.



Nam's emphasis on understanding customer needs and delivering high-touch value on top of the AI is another key strategy that sets Creatopy apart from its competitors. As large language models become ubiquitous, Creatopy differentiates itself by providing a more personalized and effective service. This approach has led to significant productivity increases for its customers, with some reporting a 10x or more increase in productivity. Nam's experience in marketing technology will be instrumental in leveraging the latest trends and technologies to enhance the product's intuitive interface and unique product capabilities, further driving customer satisfaction and retention.

However, as Creatopy continues to scale and attract larger enterprise clients, it will face several challenges. Maintaining the high level of customer service as the company grows will be a significant challenge. With an increasing number of clients, the demand for personalized and high-touch support may become difficult to meet. Nam's statement, "Some of our customers have reported a 10x or more increase in productivity because we eliminate the tedious, manual work of generating hundreds of ad variations across sizes, formats, languages, etc.," indicates the current efficiency of the service, but scaling this level of support without compromising quality will be crucial.

Another challenge is ensuring consistent brand safety across a larger and more diverse client base. As the company expands, it will need to manage a wider variety of brand guidelines and ensure that the AI-generated ads continue to adhere to these guidelines without errors. This requires continuous improvement in the AI algorithms and robust quality control processes.

In conclusion, Tammy Nam's appointment as the new CEO of Creatopy marks a new era for the company. Her extensive experience in scaling startups and her background in marketing technology position Creatopy for future growth. The company's focus on brand safety, high-touch customer service, and understanding customer needs will continue to drive its success in the competitive landscape of AI-powered marketing solutions. However, as Creatopy continues to scale, it will need to address the challenges of maintaining high levels of customer service and ensuring consistent brand safety. With Nam at the helm, Creatopy is well-positioned to navigate these challenges and achieve sustained growth.

data.title: "Warren Buffett on Debt and Donald Trump's Money Moves: Trump 'Never Went Right'"
data.description:
In the annals of financial history, few figures have been as polarizing as Donald Trump. His meteoric rise in the real estate industry, followed by a series of high-profile bankruptcies, has made him a subject of both admiration and scorn. Warren Buffett, the legendary investor and CEO of berkshire hathaway, has long been a vocal critic of Trump's financial strategies, particularly his overreliance on debt. Buffett's insights, shared during a series of lectures at Notre Dame in 1991, have resurfaced in the wake of a landmark civil fraud verdict against Trump, which ordered him to pay $355 million for fraudulently reporting the values of his properties.



Buffett's critique of Trump's financial strategies is rooted in his broader investment philosophy, which emphasizes a conservative approach to finance. In his 1991 analysis, Buffett described Trump’s overreliance on leveraging and how he went wrong with borrowing. Buffett stated, “He was terrific at borrowing money,” but he also pointed out that Trump consistently overpaid for properties by borrowing money from people, resulting in assets with no real equity. Buffett's insights highlight that Trump's financial approach was fundamentally flawed because it relied heavily on debt, which is contrary to Buffett's conservative investment philosophy.

Buffett's broader investment philosophy emphasizes a conservative approach to finance. He believes that "You don't need leverage in this world much. If you're smart, you're going to make a lot of money without borrowing." This principle is evident in Buffett's own investment strategies, where he prefers to invest in companies with strong fundamentals and avoid excessive debt. Buffett's critique of Trump's financial mismanagement is validated by Trump's history of failed ventures, such as the Trump Taj Mahal and Trump’s Plaza Hotel, both of which filed for bankruptcy in 1991 due to overleveraging and financial mismanagement. These examples support Buffett's assertion that intelligence alone does not guarantee success, and that overreliance on debt can lead to financial ruin.

The recent civil fraud verdict against Donald Trump, which ordered him to pay $355 million for fraudulently reporting the values of his properties, highlights several key financial decisions and their legal consequences that could significantly influence future investment strategies and risk management practices in the real estate industry.

Firstly, Trump's overreliance on leveraging and borrowing money to acquire properties, as critiqued by Warren Buffett in his 1991 analysis, has been a recurring theme in his financial history. Buffett noted that Trump "was terrific at borrowing money" but that his assets had no real equity in them. This overleveraging led to several of Trump's ventures, particularly in the casino and hotel industries, facing financial struggles and eventually filing for bankruptcy. For instance, the Trump Taj Mahal and Trump's Plaza Hotel both filed for bankruptcy in 1991, indicating a pattern of financial mismanagement. This pattern suggests that excessive borrowing and overleveraging can lead to significant financial risks and potential legal consequences, as seen in the recent verdict.

Secondly, the legal consequences of Trump's actions, including the $355 million fine and the prohibition from assuming the role of director in any New York-based company for three years, underscore the importance of transparency and accuracy in financial reporting. The judge's ruling, which found that Trump had misrepresented his wealth for years, emphasizes the need for real estate investors to maintain accurate and honest financial statements. This is crucial for building trust with stakeholders, including banks, insurance providers, and other financial institutions.

Thirdly, the verdict's impact on the Trump Organization, which is now under court supervision and has its business curtailed, serves as a cautionary tale for real estate companies. It highlights the potential for legal and operational disruptions that can arise from financial misconduct. This could prompt real estate firms to adopt more stringent risk management practices, including regular audits, compliance checks, and ethical guidelines to prevent similar issues.

In summary, the recent civil fraud verdict against Donald Trump serves as a stark reminder of the risks associated with overleveraging and financial misconduct in the real estate industry. It underscores the importance of conservative financial practices, accurate reporting, and robust risk management strategies to avoid legal consequences and maintain the integrity of real estate investments.

Investors can learn several crucial lessons from Trump's financial missteps, as highlighted by Warren Buffett, regarding the importance of equity, leverage, and prudent financial management.

1. The Importance of Equity: Buffett pointed out that Trump's assets had no real equity in them. This means that Trump's properties were heavily leveraged, with little to no ownership stake. Buffett's analysis underscores the importance of having equity in one's assets. Equity provides a cushion against market fluctuations and ensures that investors have a stake in their investments, reducing the risk of financial collapse. As Buffett stated, "His assets, what he paid for them, and his borrowings had no real equity in them."

2. The Risks of Overleveraging: Trump's overreliance on borrowing money to finance his properties led to significant financial troubles. Buffett noted that Trump was "terrific at borrowing money," but this strategy ultimately backfired. Overleveraging increases the risk of default, especially during economic downturns. Buffett's advice is clear: "You don't need leverage in this world much. If you're smart, you're going to make a lot of money without borrowing." This highlights the importance of maintaining a conservative approach to finance, avoiding excessive debt, and ensuring that investments are sustainable.

3. Practical Financial Management: Buffett's critique of Trump's financial management practices emphasizes the need for prudent financial management. Trump's overpaying for properties and his pattern of overleveraging led to multiple bankruptcies, including the Trump Taj Mahal and Trump's Plaza Hotel. These failures illustrate the consequences of poor financial management. Buffett's insights suggest that investors should focus on long-term sustainability and avoid rapid expansion with increased debt. As he said, "I've seen all sorts of people with terrific IQs that end up flopping in Wall Street or business because they beat themselves."

4. The Consequences of Misrepresentation: The recent ruling against Trump, where he was ordered to pay $355 million for fraudulently reporting the values of his properties, underscores the legal and financial consequences of misrepresentation. This highlights the importance of transparency and honesty in financial reporting. As Attorney General Letitia James’ lawsuit revealed, Trump and his company were found guilty of scheming to dupe banks, insurance providers, and others by inflating the former president’s wealth on financial statements. This serves as a stark reminder that financial misconduct can lead to severe penalties and legal repercussions.

In summary, investors can learn from Trump's financial missteps the importance of maintaining equity in their assets, avoiding overleveraging, practicing prudent financial management, and ensuring transparency in financial reporting. These lessons, as highlighted by Buffett, provide a roadmap for sustainable and responsible investing.

data.title: BlockDAG surges 2380% in presale Bitcoin leads crypto market at 92432 Chainlink bridges finance with 1721 Cardano offers stability at 09391 Avalanche defies competition at 2225
data.description:

The project's roadmap for 2025 includes the official main net launch, further solidifying its position as a top performer.

Bitcoin, trading around $92,432, remains the benchmark for the crypto market due to its decade-long track record and widespread acceptance. Its stability and long-term value make it a preferred choice for those seeking a reliable store of value. Bitcoin's performance often sets the tone for the broader market, making it a crucial asset for diversified portfolios.

Chainlink, trading around $17.21, is renowned for its decentralized oracle network, which bridges real-world data with blockchain technology. Its partnership with Swift highlights its potential to connect traditional finance with decentralized protocols. Despite concerns about transparency, Chainlink's innovative use case and steady community support make it a top performer for those valuing real-time data solutions.

Cardano, trading around $0.9391, is a thoroughly researched blockchain built to host decentralized applications and NFTs. Its peer-reviewed approach ensures a reliable foundation, appealing to those who value stability and academic rigor. Although slower in updates compared to competitors, Cardano's focus on scientific principles and reduced fees make it a strong contender among the best-performing crypto coins.

Avalanche, trading around $22.25, offers a blockchain platform supporting DeFi and NFTs with EVM compatibility, allowing projects to migrate from Ethereum seamlessly. Its deflationary mechanism, which burns transaction fees, and quick processing times make it attractive for those seeking fast and low-cost transactions. Despite competition from established networks, Avalanche's cross-chain flexibility and future advancements position it as a top performer.

Each of these cryptocurrencies has unique strengths that make them stand out. Bitcoin offers a time-tested store of value, Chainlink provides real-time data solutions,

data.title: Gemini Stock Surges as Winklevoss Twins Eye IPO with Goldman Sachs and Citigroup
data.description:
Crypto exchange Gemini, founded by the billionaire twins Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss, has reportedly filed for an initial public offering (IPO). The company is working with investment banking giants goldman sachs and citigroup to quietly go public. Discussions are still ongoing, and no final decision has been made concerning the IPO. An IPO is typically underwritten by investment banks, which also arrange for the shares to be listed on stock exchanges.

Last month, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) ended its probe into the exchange. In a letter addressed to Gemini lawyer Jack Baughman, the securities regulator said that it has closed its investigation and will not be pursuing any further enforcement actions against the platform. Cameron Winklevoss says the regulator’s withdrawal is a milestone that signifies the end of the SEC’s “war on crypto,” but the Gemini co-founder says this hardly makes up for the damage caused by the agency. “The SEC cost us tens of millions of dollars in legal bills alone and hundreds of millions in lost productivity, creativity and innovation.”

Gemini's IPO filing is a strategic move that could potentially attract more institutional investors to the cryptocurrency space. The involvement of Goldman Sachs and Citigroup, two of the most prestigious financial institutions, adds credibility to Gemini's public offering. These firms are known for their expertise in navigating complex financial markets and their ability to attract significant investment capital.

The IPO process is expected to be meticulously managed, given the high-profile nature of the Winklevoss twins and the regulatory environment surrounding cryptocurrencies. The confidential filing allows Gemini to gauge market interest and prepare for the public offering without the immediate scrutiny that comes with a public announcement. This approach is common among companies seeking to go public, as it provides flexibility and time to address any potential issues before the official launch.

As Gemini prepares for its IPO, the cryptocurrency industry as a whole is watching closely. The success of Gemini's public offering could set a precedent for other crypto exchanges and firms looking to enter the public markets. The involvement of major financial institutions like Goldman Sachs and Citigroup further underscores the growing acceptance of cryptocurrencies in the traditional financial sector.

data.title: Technofascism Concerns Rise as Surveillance and Manipulation Threaten Stocks
data.description:
Technofascism, a term that combines technology and fascism, has become a growing concern in recent times. This concept refers to the use of advanced technology to control and manipulate society, often in ways that infringe on individual freedoms and privacy. The idea of technofascism has been discussed in various contexts, from social media platforms to government policies, and it has sparked debates about the ethical implications of technological advancements.

One of the key concerns about technofascism is its potential to create a surveillance state. With the increasing use of artificial intelligence and data analytics, governments and corporations can monitor individuals' activities and behaviors in unprecedented ways. This level of surveillance can lead to the suppression of dissenting voices and the creation of a society where people are constantly watched and controlled.

Another aspect of technofascism is the use of technology to manipulate public opinion and influence political outcomes. Social media platforms, for example, can be used to spread misinformation and propaganda, shaping public perceptions and attitudes in ways that benefit certain interests. This manipulation can undermine democratic processes and erode trust in institutions, leading to a more authoritarian society.

Critics of technofascism argue that it is a real and present danger, and that society must take steps to prevent its further development. Some suggest that increased regulation and oversight of technology companies is necessary to ensure that they operate in the public interest. Others call for greater transparency and accountability in the use of data and surveillance technologies, so that individuals can have more control over their personal information.

Despite these concerns, there are also those who believe that technology can be used to promote democracy and human rights. They argue that by harnessing the power of technology, society can create more open and inclusive systems that empower individuals and communities. However, this requires a commitment to ethical principles and a willingness to address the challenges posed by technofascism.

In conclusion, technofascism is a complex and multifaceted issue that raises important questions about the role of technology in society. While it presents significant challenges, it also offers opportunities for positive change. By taking a proactive approach to addressing these issues, society can ensure that technology is used in ways that promote freedom, democracy, and human rights.

data.title: "Is The Kroger Co. (KR) the Best Performing Dividend Stock to Buy Now?"
data.description:
In the ever-evolving landscape of dividend stocks, one name that consistently stands out is the kroger co. (KR). With a dividend yield of 1.92% and a market cap of $47.62 billion, kroger is not just the largest grocery supermarket chain in the United States but also a formidable player in the dividend game. But is it the best performing dividend stock to buy now? Let's dive in and find out.



First, let's look at the numbers. Kroger's annual dividend of $1.28 per share, paid every three months, provides a steady income stream for investors. The last ex-dividend date was February 14, 2025, indicating a consistent dividend payment schedule. This stability is crucial for income-focused investors who rely on regular payouts.

But how does Kroger's dividend yield compare to its competitors? sprouts farmers market (SFM) has a dividend yield of 0.00%, Ingles Markets (IMKTA) has a yield of 1.09%, and Walmart (WMT) has a yield of 0.85%. Kroger's 1.92% yield is not only higher than its competitors but also competitive within the broader market, which typically ranges from 1.5% to 2%.

Now, let's talk about financial performance. In 2024, Kroger's revenue was $147.12 billion, a slight decrease from the previous year's $150.04 billion. However, earnings were $2.67 billion, an increase of 24.18%. This earnings growth is a positive sign for dividend sustainability, as it provides the company with the financial flexibility to maintain and potentially increase its dividend payments.

But it's not just about the numbers. Kroger's dividend policy has evolved significantly over the years. The dividend yield has fluctuated, but it has shown a pattern of growth. For example, in 2024, the dividend yield was 2.00%, and in 2023, it was 2.41%. This indicates that Kroger has been able to maintain a consistent dividend yield over time, which is a positive sign for dividend investors.

However, it's not all sunshine and rainbows. Kroger's recent CEO resignation due to personal conduct issues has raised some concerns. The company's fiscal 2025 projection falls short of analyst estimates, and the latest big store chain to issue downbeat guidance. This could impact investor confidence and potentially affect the stock price.

But let's not forget the bigger picture. Kroger's strong financial performance, consistent dividend payments, and competitive dividend yield make it a strong dividend stock. The company's commitment to returning value to shareholders through its dividend policy is evident in its historical data and industry benchmarks.

In conclusion, while there are some concerns, Kroger's strong dividend yield, consistent dividend growth, and stable financial performance make it a strong dividend stock. Whether it's the best performing dividend stock to buy now depends on your investment goals and risk tolerance. But one thing is for sure: Kroger is a company worth watching in the dividend game.

data.title: PEPE Plummets 15% Weekly as Key Support Breaks, Whales Unload Trillions
data.description:
PEPE, the third-largest meme token, has recently lost a critical support level of $0.00000770, raising concerns about its future trajectory. This breakdown has extended its weekly losses to 15%, indicating a potential deeper crash. The memecoin sector, despite overall volatility, has seen a surge of 7.07%, pushing its total market value to $57.64 billion. However, while high-cap memecoins face sell pressure, mid and low-cap tokens are outperforming with double-digit gains, suggesting a shift in liquidity toward riskier, high-reward plays.

PEPE's network activity has significantly declined, with active addresses dropping from 28,000 at its post-election peak to just 2,573. This reduction in participation reflects weakening investor confidence and increases the risk of further downside. The 1D chart shows no clear accumulation, as each dip has been met with more selling pressure. A 43% decline in volume reinforces uncertainty, making $0.000006477 a fragile support level. Without a demand resurgence, PEPE risks an extended breakdown, with the next key support yet to be established.

Currently, 78.39% of PEPE holders are underwater, with a key cluster of buyers between $0.000006 and $0.000007 now at a breaking point. If selling pressure persists, PEPE could slide further into lower liquidity zones, with no clear support on its 1D chart. This weak demand structure exposes the memecoin to deeper corrections. Nearly 13,400 addresses holding over 20 trillion PEPE at the minimum entry of $0.0000060 face the risk of slipping into losses. If this zone fails, the memecoin may retrace further, testing lower liquidity pockets.

Meanwhile, a key whale wallet holding between 100 million and 1 billion PEPE has entered a heavy distribution phase, unloading over a trillion tokens. Its holdings have dropped from 13.83 trillion to 12.6 trillion, with no signs of accumulation. data.title: Stocks mixed after jobs report, unemployment ticks up
"Angel Investor's Cautionary Advice for LGBTQ+ Founders"
data.description:
In the world of venture capital, the underfunding of LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs is a stark reminder of the systemic barriers that persist despite their exceptional performance. Despite comprising 7.1% of the U.S. population, LGBTQ+ founders received only 0.5% of the $2.1 trillion in U.S. startup funding from 2000 to 2022. This disparity is not just a financial issue; it's a moral one. It's a testament to the biases and prejudices that still plague the investment landscape, where LGBTQ+ founders are often seen as risky investments simply because of their identity.



The data is clear: LGBTQ+ founders outperform their peers in various metrics. They have created 36% more jobs, secured 114% more patents, and achieved 44% more exits compared to their peers. This exceptional performance with highly limited resources demonstrates their efficiency and effectiveness in creating value—a clear signal to investors that these entrepreneurs are not just competitive but high-potential investment opportunities. Yet, they continue to face significant challenges in securing angel investments.

One of the key challenges is the pervasive discrimination and bias within the investment community. LGBTQ+ founders often conceal their sexual orientation to avoid discrimination, with 75% of founders hiding their identity just to work, let alone to start a company. This concealment is a direct result of the systemic barriers that LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs face in accessing capital. It's a vicious cycle: they need funding to scale and innovate, but they can't get the funding because of their identity.

To address these challenges and foster a more inclusive investment environment, both public and private sectors must take proactive steps. Government initiatives, such as grants and funding specifically for LGBTQ+ businesses, can help level the playing field. For example, dedicated funds for LGBTQ+ founders, investor participation in community events, and public declarations of support can make a significant difference in creating an environment where these entrepreneurs feel empowered to seek the resources they need.

Private investors also need to adopt more inclusive practices, such as integrating LGBTQ+ metrics into their investment criteria and actively supporting LGBTQ+ founders through mentorship and community engagement. True allyship goes beyond words; it requires tangible support, including financial investment and the creation of inclusive networks that allow LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs to thrive. For example, Chasing Rainbows, a venture capital fund, exclusively invests in LGBTQ+-owned businesses and has been very visible in the venture ecosystem as a fund which specifically invests in LGBTQ+ founded companies. This visibility and targeted investment strategy have helped to create a more inclusive investment environment for LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs.

Moreover, post-investment support is crucial for maximizing returns. LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs often face unique challenges that require tailored support. Investors who provide not just capital, but also strategic guidance and mentorship can help LGBTQ+ founders navigate these challenges and scale their businesses more effectively. An effective approach is to design and deploy a matrix approach with subject matter experts (SME) by industries and skill sets, supporting these start-ups on a fractional or as-needed basis. Mentorship from experienced investors who understand the specific needs of LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs can also be transformative. This includes helping them build networks, access new markets, and develop strategies that leverage their unique insights and perspectives. Such support not only increases the chances of business success but also strengthens the investor-founder relationship, fostering long-term collaboration and trust.

In summary, investing in LGBTQ+-led startups is about more than just social responsibility; it’s a sound financial move that statistically leads to outsized returns. Studies have shown that diverse teams bring a broader range of perspectives, leading to better decision-making, innovation, and, hence, higher success rates. This is particularly relevant in industries where disruption and creativity are keys to success, such as fintech, digital health, clean energy, and media—all areas where LGBTQ+ founders are contributing actively and economically. By supporting LGBTQ+ entrepreneurs, investors can align financial returns with social progress, creating a win-win scenario for investors, society, and the economy at large.

data.title: U.S NFP Slightly Misses; Unemployment May Rise Further
data.description:
The U.S. added 151,000 jobs in February, slightly below the expected 160,000. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate rose to 4.1%, the highest since November 2024, exceeding both forecasts and January's 4.0%.

Average hourly earnings grew 0.3% month-over-month, in line with expectations.

After NFP report, U.S stock futures initially jumped after the report but later erased gains, turning negative as bulls and bears fiercely contested market direction. Wall Street opened slightly lower, with stocks attempting a modest rebound in early trading.

The U.S. dollar index (DXY) fell sharply, dropping 0.56% to 103.6, approaching its lowest level since November 2024. A break below this level could bring a direct test of the 100 mark.

Gold remained stable, while silver dropped nearly 2%, pressured by weaker employment data given its industrial demand component.

Sector Breakdown: Healthcare Strong, Retail and Hospitality Weak

Healthcare led job gains with +52,000 new positions. Transportation & warehousing added +18,000 jobs, emerging as another key driver. Retail lost 6,000 jobs, while leisure & hospitality declined by 16,000, reflecting weak consumer demand. 

Federal government employment saw its steepest drop since June 2022 due to Musk's aggressive efficiency reforms.

Fed Rate Cut Expectations Adjusted

Following the NFP release, markets scaled back expectations for a Fed rate cut in May, now favoring June as the likely start of policy easing.

Despite heightened uncertainty from Trump's economic policies, traders still expect 75 basis points of rate cuts in 2025.

Analysts' Views: Federal Job Cuts to Weigh on Future Reports

1️⃣ Government Layoffs Yet to Show Full Impact

Economists believe Musk's government workforce reductions have not fully appeared in February's NFP. Third-party data suggests that 63,583 U.S. government employees were laid off in February. Unemployment claims among federal workers have already risen. In addition to direct layoffs, Trump's hiring freeze (effective Jan 20) has frozen thousands of public-sector job openings.

2️⃣ Spending Drops in Washington D.C.

Government job cuts have led to a decline in consumer spending. bank of america data shows a notable slowdown in credit card spending in Washington D.C., where federal employees are concentrated.

3️⃣ Corporate Layoffs & Economic Pressure on Workers

Goldman Sachs and disney recently announced large-scale layoffs, which were not included in February's unemployment rate. Along with government job losses, this could drive higher unemployment in the coming months. 

The latest data shows: Low-education workers (without a high school diploma) were hit hardest. Part-time employment due to economic hardship surged to its highest level in four years.

Long-Term Impact: Job Market Faces Structural Shifts

Government Cuts & Immigration Policies Could Suppress Hiring

Musk's government downsizing, reduced spending, and Trump's strict immigration policies could limit job growth. However, some businesses praise Trump's tax cuts and deregulation, which may boost hiring. The White House argues that tariffs will bring back U.S. manufacturing jobs, while government streamlining will enhance long-term efficiency.

Inflation and Consumer Spending: A New Risk

Sticky inflation remains a concern, and consumers are cutting back on spending. If inflation persists and consumer demand weakens, companies may further slow hiring.

Final Thoughts

While February's job growth slightly missed expectations, rising unemployment and government layoffs could pose larger risks in the coming months. The labor market is at a turning point, with policy shifts, economic uncertainty, and corporate layoffs shaping the outlook.
